summary.title: Professional Summary
summary.intro: I'm Manikanta Engalligi — a Master's student in Computer Science specializing in Big Data & Artificial Intelligence at SRH University Leipzig, Germany.
summary.p1: I build scalable software across full-stack development, artificial intelligence, machine learning, and data analytics, turning complex ideas into reliable, production-ready systems.
summary.p2: I'm driven by solving real-world problems with technology and continuously sharpening my craft through modern software engineering practices.

vital-guard.title: Vital Guard – IoT Safety System
vital-guard.description: IoT-based wearable fall detection system using ESP8266, MPU6050, GPS and Blynk.
vital-guard.impact: Enables instant, location-aware fall alerts so help can reach at-risk users faster.
vital-guard.overview: A wearable safety device that automatically detects falls and alerts caregivers in real time with the wearer's live location.
vital-guard.problem: Elderly and at-risk individuals who fall while alone often cannot call for help, turning a manageable incident into a medical emergency.
vital-guard.solution: An ESP8266-based wearable continuously reads motion data from an MPU6050 sensor, detects fall patterns, captures GPS coordinates and pushes instant alerts to caregivers through the Blynk platform.
achievements: Real-time fall detection using motion-pattern thresholds
Instant caregiver alerts with live GPS location
Low-power wearable design suitable for all-day use
vital-guard.architecture: A sensor layer (MPU6050 + GPS) feeds the ESP8266 microcontroller, which runs the detection logic and communicates over Wi-Fi with the Blynk cloud for notifications and dashboards.
challenges: Tuning thresholds to minimise false positives
Maintaining reliable connectivity on a low-power device
futureImprovements: On-device ML model for smarter fall classification

video-anomaly-detection.title: Video Anomaly Detection System
video-anomaly-detection.description: AI-powered surveillance anomaly detection using transfer learning and deep learning techniques.
video-anomaly-detection.impact: Reduces manual surveillance monitoring through automated, frame-level anomaly detection.
video-anomaly-detection.overview: An AI surveillance system that automatically flags unusual or suspicious activity in video streams.
video-anomaly-detection.problem: Manual monitoring of surveillance footage is tedious and error-prone, so critical anomalies are frequently missed.
video-anomaly-detection.solution: Using transfer learning on convolutional neural networks, the system learns normal patterns from video frames (processed with OpenCV) and flags deviations as anomalies.
achievements: Transfer learning to reduce training-data requirements
Frame-level anomaly scoring on live video
video-anomaly-detection.architecture: OpenCV handles frame extraction and preprocessing; a CNN built with TensorFlow performs feature extraction and anomaly scoring, with results surfaced for review.
challenges: Handling varied lighting and camera conditions
Balancing detection sensitivity against false alarms

sms-spam-detection.title: SMS Spam Detection System
sms-spam-detection.description: NLP-based machine learning system for classifying spam and legitimate messages.
sms-spam-detection.impact: Delivers high-accuracy spam filtering with a lightweight, fast NLP pipeline.
sms-spam-detection.overview: A machine-learning system that classifies text messages as spam or legitimate (ham).
sms-spam-detection.problem: Spam messages clutter inboxes and expose users to phishing and fraud.
sms-spam-detection.solution: Messages are cleaned and tokenised with NLTK, transformed into numerical features, and classified using scikit-learn models trained on labelled SMS data.
sms-spam-detection.architecture: Text preprocessing (NLTK) → feature extraction (TF-IDF) → scikit-learn classifier → prediction, wrapped in a reusable pipeline.
challenges: Avoiding overfitting on short, noisy text
